title: The History of Tea
description: For questions 9 – 16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only one word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Tea is (0) the second most consumed beverage in the world after water, with a history spanning over 5,000 years. The story of tea begins in ancient China, where legend credits Emperor Shen Nung with its discovery around 2737 BCE.||According (9) to the legend, the emperor was boiling water under a tea tree when leaves fell into his pot, creating the first cup of tea. From China, tea cultivation and consumption spread (10) to Japan, where it became central to Japanese culture through elaborate tea ceremonies. Buddhist monks played an important role (11) in spreading tea throughout Asia, using it to stay alert during long meditation sessions.||Tea arrived in Europe during (12) the 17th century through Portuguese and Dutch traders. It quickly became fashionable among the aristocracy, particularly in Britain, (13) where afternoon tea became a beloved tradition. The British Empire's influence helped establish tea plantations in India and Ceylon (now Sri Lanka), (14) which remain major tea producers today.||Different regions have developed their own unique tea cultures and preparation methods. (15) Whether you prefer Chinese green tea, Indian chai, or British breakfast tea, this ancient beverage continues to bring people together across cultures. Today, tea (16) is enjoyed by billions of people worldwide in countless varieties and preparations.

example.text: Tea is (0) second most consumed beverage in the world after water.

questions.explanation: Expresión fija: 'according to the legend' significa 'según la leyenda'.
Verbo 'spread to' indica expansión hacia un lugar.
Expresión 'play a role in' indica participación en un proceso.
Artículo definido con siglos: 'during the 17th century'.
Pronombre relativo de lugar que se refiere a Gran Bretaña.
Pronombre relativo que se refiere a India y Sri Lanka.
Conjunción 'Whether' introduce alternativas de preferencias de té.
Voz pasiva presente: 'is enjoyed' describe situación actual.
